canonical_definition: The latent capability of a multilingual LLM to perform accurately in a particular language, reflecting differences in training data coverage and model fluency.
papers: Languages Still Left Behind: Toward a Better Multilingual Machine Translation Benchmark

definitions.text: The latent capability of a multilingual LLM to perform accurately in a particular language, reflecting differences in training data coverage and model fluency.
The tendency for a model to exhibit differential performance on a task depending on the linguistic modality of the input, even when the content is similar.
definitions.paper: Languages Still Left Behind: Toward a Better Multilingual Machine Translation Benchmark

characterization: The latent capability of a multilingual LLM to perform accurately in a particular language, reflecting differences in training data coverage and model fluency.

emnlp25: Languages Still Left Behind: Toward a Better Multilingual Machine Translation Benchmark
